name: t3-code-ultralight-browser-fork
purpose: Let a local web, canvas, voice, or custom interface talk to the user's authenticated local Codex.

verification.optionalNpmPublish: enabled only when NPM_PUBLISH_ENABLED is true and the repository NPM_TOKEN secret is configured

workspace.default: directory where setup, start, or serve is invoked
workspace.reusePolicy: exact normalized workspace and Codex-binary fingerprints

completeChat.useWhen: The host wants a complete isolated chat UI.
completeChat.hostCommandResponses: acknowledgements and thread or turn IDs only; never prompt or response text
completeChat.hostCommandOriginPolicy: exact parent window plus the bridge's effective browser-origin allowlist

customUi.useWhen: The host already owns a canvas, voice, spatial, game, or bespoke interface.

attachedServer.useWhen: An existing Node HTTP server should own the bridge lifecycle.

acceptance: doctor reports ok true
bridge status is ready
one real local Codex turn streams through the final user-facing UI
stop and approval paths remain operable
no Codex credential or token is copied into browser code
an unlisted browser origin is rejected even when it is another localhost or loopback page
an allowed loopback browser host streams while an unlisted sibling is denied hosted modules, WebSocket, iframe commands, and framing
a fresh packed consumer compiles and imports every declared package export
the packed agent-prompt contains the exact versioned release archive, all host routes, lifecycle wiring, security constraints, and final streamed-turn verification
an allowed HTTPS non-loopback host streams while an unlisted sibling origin fails HTTP modules and WebSocket upgrades
a trusted file origin streams only with the explicit null allowlist and matching CORS
a complete-chat host can send before iframe readiness, receive only IDs, stop, and start a clean thread without iframe DOM access
complete-chat commands from an unlisted or spoofed parent origin are ignored without acknowledgement
setup from a fresh project directory makes that directory the bridge default without embedding its path in browser status
a bridge started for one workspace is not silently reused as another project's default
a custom --codex binary is used for diagnostics and the background app-server while a different binary cannot silently reuse that bridge
the setup receipt supplies installed and zero-install lifecycle commands that restore the exact bridge port, workspace, binary, and origin policy after a full stop
setup-generated browser code inherits the bridge workspace without embedding an absolute local path
the exact packed React export server-renders and streams in clean React 18 and React 19 hosts
the exact packed React export builds in Next.js 16 App Router, imports directly from a Server Component, and streams through the production host
the exact packed Web Component streams in clean Vue 3 and Svelte 5 hosts with one mount initialization and pre-ready controller calls
the exact packed attached-server export streams on its custom path without touching the host's HTTP or WebSocket routes
attached shutdown rejects pending owned requests before process exit and terminates non-cooperative browser sockets within its configured bound
automatic setup coexists with another project's default bridge, repeats on the same deterministic port, and returns exact runtime URLs
the exact generated hosted assistant recipe uses one import, streams a stateful follow-up, cancels an active turn, and disposes its scoped fail-closed request adapter
published release archives are rebuilt from the tag, checked, audited, npm-dry-run verified, provenance-attested, and replaced by GitHub Actions
source and packed-consumer checks pass independently on every supported Node.js major
source and packed-consumer checks pass on Ubuntu, macOS, and Windows at the minimum supported Node.js major
a disposable Codex-compatible process passes version, app-server initialization, account, model, thread-store, and shutdown checks on every supported operating system
setup, detached launch, a streamed browser-client turn, status, stop, exact-port restart, and a post-restart turn pass on every supported operating system
the standalone browser app remains within 110000 decoded JavaScript and CSS bytes
